title: Sharm El Sheikh 5-Day Beach Getaway
metaTitle: Sharm El Sheikh 5-Day Beach Getaway | Flights, Hotels & Tours Included
metaDescription: Book a 5-day trip to Sharm El Sheikh, Egypt: round-trip flights, hotel, breakfast, transfers, and guided tours including Ras Mohammed National Park snorkeling/diving and Tiran Island snorkeling boat trip. Prices from $1280 per person (estimate).
highlights: Scuba diving Ras Mohammed and the SS Thistlegorm wreck
Private yacht snorkeling charter to Tiran Island
Sunset dinner cruise on the Red Sea
St. Catherine's Monastery & Mount Sinai sunrise hike
overview: Spend 5 days discovering Sharm El Sheikh, Egypt — Hot desert climate with a coastal moderating effect — very hot, dry summers (June-August, highs 38-40°C) and mild, pleasant winters (December-February, highs around 22-23°C). Rain is virtually nonexistent year-round. March-May and October-November offer warm, comfortable temperatures for diving and sightseeing without the extreme summer heat of June-August. This itinerary blends Ras Mohammed National Park snorkeling/diving, Tiran Island snorkeling boat trip, Na'ama Bay promenade with free time to explore at your own pace, backed by round-trip flights, handpicked accommodation, and a dedicated travel consultant from enquiry through departure.
included: Round-trip economy-class airfare from your selected origin city (upgrade to premium economy or business available)
4 nights accommodation in your selected category (Budget, Mid-range, Premium, or Luxury)
Daily local transport as per the itinerary
Guided tours including Ras Mohammed National Park snorkeling/diving, Tiran Island snorkeling boat trip, Na'ama Bay promenade
All entrance fees for attractions and activities listed in the itinerary
Applicable taxes and OTA service fee
Dedicated travel consultant support from booking through departure
notIncluded: Visa fees and travel document costs (see Visa Information)
Travel insurance (strongly recommended, available as an add-on)
Lunches and dinners except where noted in the itinerary
Optional activities not listed in the day-by-day itinerary
Airline seat selection and baggage upgrades
Any fare difference for premium economy or business class upgrades

itinerary.title: Arrive in Sharm El Sheikh
itinerary.description: Land at Sharm El Sheikh International Airport (SSH) and take your included private transfer to your hotel. After settling in, ease into the trip with a relaxed visit to Ras Mohammed National Park snorkeling/diving — One of the world's top dive sites at the tip of the Sinai Peninsula, famous coral walls. Round off the evening with a welcome dinner introducing you to local flavors.

itinerary.title: Sharm El Sheikh: Tiran Island snorkeling boat trip & Blue Hole diving (Dahab day trip)
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Tiran Island snorkeling boat trip — Vivid coral reefs in the Straits of Tiran between Sinai and Saudi Arabia. In the afternoon, continue to Blue Hole diving (Dahab day trip) — World-famous deep-water diving sinkhole. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Sharm El Sheikh: Colored Canyon & St. Catherine's Monastery tour & Quad biking / desert safari with Bedouin dinner
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Colored Canyon & St. Catherine's Monastery tour — Desert canyon hike and visit to one of the world's oldest working monasteries. In the afternoon, continue to Quad biking / desert safari with Bedouin dinner — Sunset desert excursion with traditional Bedouin hospitality. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.
activities: Colored Canyon & St. Catherine's Monastery tour
Quad biking / desert safari with Bedouin dinner

itinerary.title: Day Trip to St. Catherine's Monastery & Mount Sinai sunrise hike
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to St. Catherine's Monastery & Mount Sinai sunrise hike, a popular side trip from Sharm El Sheikh. Return to Sharm El Sheikh in the evening with time free to explore on your own.
activities: Guided day trip: St. Catherine's Monastery & Mount Sinai sunrise hike

itinerary.title: Departure Day
itinerary.description: Enjoy a final morning at leisure — perhaps one last visit to SOHO Square entertainment complex or some souvenir shopping — before your private transfer to SSH for your departure flight.

travelTips: Always agree a taxi fare before the ride begins — meters are uncommon.
Book diving trips with PADI/SSI-certified operators, especially for Ras Mohammed and wreck dives.
Bring cash in small denominations for tipping (baksheesh), which is expected throughout the service industry.
The Red Sea reefs here are among the best-preserved in the world — use reef-safe sunscreen and never touch coral.
Desert safari evenings can get cool — bring a light jacket even in summer.
Pace yourself — this itinerary includes free time so you are not rushing between every attraction.
packingList: Copies of booking confirmations and travel insurance
Lightweight, breathable clothing and a reusable water bottle
Swimwear and snorkel gear (or rent locally)
Reef-safe sunscreen — the Red Sea reefs are ecologically sensitive
A light cover-up for visits beyond the resort strip
thingsToDo: Colored Canyon & St. Catherine's Monastery tour
Quad biking / desert safari with Bedouin dinner
Sharm el Sheikh Old Town streets
Scuba diving Ras Mohammed and the SS Thistlegorm wreck
thingsToAvoid: Touching or standing on coral while snorkeling or diving — it's slow-growing and easily damaged.
Getting into a taxi without agreeing the fare first.
Drinking tap water — stick to bottled water, provided at nearly all resorts.
Wandering into the desert independently without a guide — stick to organized excursions.
topRestaurants: Fares Seafood — Fresh Red Sea Seafood ($$)
El Fanar Restaurant — Egyptian & Mediterranean (clifftop views) ($$$)
Old Market kushari stalls — Egyptian Street Food ($)
nightlife: SOHO Square evening fountains and bars
instagramSpots: Ras Mohammed coral reef walls underwater
Na'ama Bay palm-lined promenade at sunset
St. Catherine's Monastery against Mount Sinai
hiddenGems: Ras Abu Galum protected bay (accessible by camel or boat from Dahab)
adventureActivities: Scuba diving Ras Mohammed and the SS Thistlegorm wreck
luxuryExperiences: Private yacht snorkeling charter to Tiran Island
Liveaboard dive boat to the Straits of Tiran and Ras Mohammed
Suite stay at a Nabq Bay 5-star resort
honeymoonExperiences: Sunset dinner cruise on the Red Sea
Private beach cabana at a couples-focused resort
Snorkeling together at Ras Mohammed's coral gardens
budgetExperiences: Old Market wandering and street food
Na'ama Bay promenade — free to walk
Snorkeling directly off many public beach areas

festivalCalendar.name: Sharm El Sheikh International Film Festival
Eid al-Fitr and Eid al-Adha celebrations

localEtiquette: Modest dress is appreciated away from resort pools and beaches, especially in Old Market/Na'ama Bay town areas.
Tipping (baksheesh) is customary and expected for most services, from dive guides to hotel staff.
Bargaining is standard practice at markets and with taxi fares — always agree a price before starting a ride.
Friday is the main day of prayer; some shops and services may have adjusted hours.
Public displays of affection should be kept minimal outside of resort areas.
localTransportGuide: Sharm El Sheikh's resort areas (Na'ama Bay, Nabq Bay, Sharm el Maya) are spread along the coast, so taxis (always agree a fare beforehand) or hotel shuttles are the main way to get around. Most excursions — diving, desert safaris, day trips — include pickup and drop-off from your hotel.
bestTimeToVisit: March-May and October-November offer warm, comfortable temperatures for diving and sightseeing without the extreme summer heat of June-August.
weather: Hot desert climate with a coastal moderating effect — very hot, dry summers (June-August, highs 38-40°C) and mild, pleasant winters (December-February, highs around 22-23°C). Rain is virtually nonexistent year-round.
visaInformation: Most Western nationalities including the US, UK, EU, and Australian citizens can obtain a visa-on-arrival or e-Visa; a free Sinai-only entry stamp (up to 15 days) is available for visitors staying solely within the Sharm El Sheikh/Sinai region. Verify current requirements against your specific passport before travel.
averageFlightTime: ~9.8h from major hubs (estimate; varies by origin — see Suggested Flights)
cancellationPolicy: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.
termsAndConditions: Pricing shown is per person based on the traveler type and tier selected and is an estimate pending live fare and rate confirmation.
Passports must be valid for at least 6 months beyond the return date for most destinations — confirm the exact requirement for your nationality.
Itinerary order may change due to weather, local conditions, or attraction closures; inclusions remain equivalent.
Solo travelers incur a single-room supplement, reflected in the Solo pricing tier.
This package does not include international travel insurance unless explicitly listed under Included.
Prices shown in a currency other than USD are approximate conversions at the rate noted and are not a payment quote.

faqs.question: Do I need a visa to visit Sharm El Sheikh?
faqs.answer: Most Western nationalities including the US, UK, EU, and Australian citizens can obtain a visa-on-arrival or e-Visa; a free Sinai-only entry stamp (up to 15 days) is available for visitors staying solely within the Sharm El Sheikh/Sinai region. Verify current requirements against your specific passport before travel.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit Sharm El Sheikh?
faqs.answer: March-May and October-November offer warm, comfortable temperatures for diving and sightseeing without the extreme summer heat of June-August. Hot desert climate with a coastal moderating effect — very hot, dry summers (June-August, highs 38-40°C) and mild, pleasant winters (December-February, highs around 22-23°C). Rain is virtually nonexistent year-round.

faqs.question: What's included in this package?
faqs.answer: Round-trip airfare, accommodation, daily breakfast, airport transfers, daily local transport, and guided tours to the attractions listed in the itinerary. See the Included / Not Included sections for full detail.

faqs.question: Can the itinerary be customized?
faqs.answer: Yes — this itinerary is a starting point. Your travel consultant can add, remove, or reorder activities, extend your stay, or upgrade your hotel category before you book.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.

faqs.question: Is Sharm El Sheikh safe for tourists?
faqs.answer: Sharm El Sheikh is generally safe for tourists who take standard precautions — avoid poorly lit areas at night, keep valuables secure, and use licensed transport.

faqs.question: What currency should I bring to Sharm El Sheikh?
faqs.answer: The local currency is EGP. Carry some cash for smaller vendors — card acceptance can be inconsistent outside major hotels and malls.

faqs.question: Is this package good for a honeymoon or family trip?
faqs.answer: Yes — Sharm El Sheikh is one of our most-booked honeymoon destinations, especially the Sunset dinner cruise on the Red Sea experience. It also works well for families — highlights include Glass-bottom boat tours.

reviews.author: Sarah Andersen
reviews.title: Best vacation we've had
reviews.body: Sharm El Sheikh is stunning and this package covered the essentials well, especially Tiran Island snorkeling boat trip. The hotel could have been a little closer to the center but transport was easy.

reviews.author: Priya Patel
reviews.title: Great for families with kids
reviews.body: Booked this as a couple trip and it was well paced. Ras Mohammed National Park snorkeling/diving in particular stood out. A couple of the transfers ran a little late but nothing that spoiled the trip.

reviews.author: Elena Brown
reviews.title: Great for families with kids
reviews.body: We added a couple of optional activities beyond Na'ama Bay promenade and don't regret it. Good value for what was included, and the food recommendations from our guide were spot on.

reviews.author: David Garcia
reviews.title: Best vacation we've had
reviews.body: Sharm El Sheikh is stunning and this package covered the essentials well, especially Blue Hole diving (Dahab day trip). The hotel could have been a little closer to the center but transport was easy.

reviews.author: Olivia Rossi
reviews.title: Exceeded our expectations
reviews.body: Our trip to Sharm El Sheikh was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to Tiran Island snorkeling boat trip was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.

reviews.author: Elena Botha
reviews.title: Five stars, will return
reviews.body: We added a couple of optional activities beyond SOHO Square entertainment complex and don't regret it. Good value for what was included, and the food recommendations from our guide were spot on.

reviews.author: Hiroshi Santos
reviews.title: Would book again in a heartbeat
reviews.body: Old Market (Sharm el Maya) alone was worth the trip, but combined with Old Market (Sharm el Maya) this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.

reviews.author: Olivia Brown
reviews.title: Loved every moment
reviews.body: Our trip to Sharm El Sheikh was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to Ras Mohammed National Park snorkeling/diving was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.

reviews.body: SOHO Square entertainment complex alone was worth the trip, but combined with Ras Um Sid public viewpoint this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.
